Author: mithro_
Content: @Leo Moser (mole99) - Forgive me if these things are stupid / don't make sense but I had some random thoughts / ideas about ways we should probably modify the "default" template / setup for the padring / pads based on random theories from being at the bond house.

Author: mithro_
Content:
Firstly, the pads seem like they are currently very close to the edge of the die. Looking through the microscope they kinda look like they almost connected to it. I assume they must not be, otherwise nothing would work but it was concerning the first time I looked at them.

Could we add a bit more space between the pads and the edge/seal ring?

I know it reduces the available silicon area but I /think/ it should help have more margin there.

Author: mithro_
Content: Secondly, do you know if the top polyimide layer overlaps the metal pad edges? IE Is something like this possible? https://docs.google.com/drawings/d/1YPRHE-uZNg3iSm3SlMcJvfinUueOHtiQbLqJo35Pt_M/edit -- If so could we increase the amount of overlap?

Author: mole99
Content: Will take a closer look later!

Author: 246tnt
Content: Changing padring would mean we have an issue for TT because changing silicon area means we might need to change the size of tiles (and worst case, even size of power gates meaning we'd need to re-design them ... meaning closing the shuttle)

Author: 246tnt
Content: The passivation already looks like that drawing. The metal pad is 65.35 um x 68 um  and tha passivation opening is 60 x 60 um.

Author: 246tnt
Content: Distance from guard ring to pads meets `GR.2` ( It's 10 um from the marking layer and 11 um away from the actual pad ).   That's more than twice the clearance between pad and first power ring.

Author: mithro_
Content: @tnt - I'm thinking more along the lines of providing more margin in the "default - I just want something which works" configuration of the template.

Author: 246tnt
Content: But that would move the bonding pads, hence the bonding setup.

Author: mithro_
Content: But I also might be suggesting things which make no sense 😛

Author: mithro_
Content: @tnt - If I understand correctly, Tiny Tapeout/you are doing your own bonding (even if we are at the same place) - so that is not something that effects you?

Author: polyfractal
Content:
parallel lapping and cross-sectioning my chip are on the eventual-todo list (time permitting, have to do it after-hours at work)

What failure in particular would I be looking out for?

Author: 246tnt
Content: @BreakingTaps Bond pad shorted to GND.

Author: azonenberg
Content: is it just one pin on one chip? wire bonded or bare?

Author: 246tnt
Content: @azonenberg No, I have 9 chips here, 6 have several failed bonds.

Author: azonenberg
Content: but randomly distributed, not the same bond pad on each?

Author: azonenberg
Content: any obvious pattern to failure locations?

Author: azonenberg
Content: e.g. power vs io

Author: azonenberg
Content: or one side of the package

Author: 246tnt
Content: It's not the same bond no. But strangely they seem grouped on the same side of the chip ... it's not the same side on every chip but on a given chip I'll have 1 side perfect and another with all the failed bonds.

Author: 246tnt
Content: ( I'm only testing 2 sides because the samples I got have "survivor bias", they were the one passing the digital test which tests North/South side at the factory ... but the East/West were not tested at the factory ).

Author: azonenberg
Content: My first guess would be excessive force during the bonding process punching through layers

Author: azonenberg
Content: That should show nicely in a FIB section

Author: 246tnt
Content: If @BreakingTaps has a multimeter, it's pretty easy to test .. Use diode more, put positive lead on GND on one of the cap, then probe along the connector for the IOs.  Good ones should show roughly 0.75V.

Author: azonenberg
Content: How wide is the bond pad?

Author: 246tnt
Content: @azonenberg There is nothing underneath the pad. It would  need to be deformed to push inward to reach the power ring to be shorted to GND.

Author: azonenberg
Content: Substrate is grounded if it's punched hard enough

Author: 246tnt
Content: True ... I didn't think about that.

Author: 246tnt
Content: I did manage to clear a short using brute force. ( 8V / 3A to the IO ... now it works )

Author: 246tnt
Content: Pad passivation opening is  60 x 60 um.

Author: 246tnt
Content: And as noted above, ~4.6 um from the GND power rail.

Content: Yes, rest of the chip works. And that pin now works too ( it was the `clk` input of TT ) ... I ran several designs with no issues.
